title: Update Company Snapshot currency/percentage symbols and snapshot labels

requirements.summary: Switch Company Snapshot currency labeling and currency-format helpers from USD ($) to INR (₹).
acceptanceCriteria: In the Company Snapshot form, the labels for Market Cap, Last Traded Price, Revenue Last Year, Net Profit Last Year, Revenue Last Quarter, and Net Profit Last Quarter show "(₹)" instead of "($)".
Any frontend formatting utility used to format currency amounts outputs INR (₹) instead of USD ($) (including large-number formatting if used).
No "$" currency symbol remains visible in the Company Snapshot section after the change (except inside user-entered values).

file_operations.description: Update Company Snapshot currency field labels/help text to use rupee symbol "(₹)" instead of "($)" for Market Cap, Last Traded Price, Revenue/Net Profit (Last Year/Last Quarter).
Update currency-formatting helpers to output INR (₹) instead of USD ($), including any large-number currency abbreviations so they no longer hardcode "$".

requirements.summary: Convert Promoter Pledge Quantity to a percentage field with % label, percent-style parsing/display, and 0–100% validation.
acceptanceCriteria: The Company Snapshot field label reads "Promoter Pledge Quantity (%)" (spelled correctly as "Promoter").

The input behavior matches existing percentage fields (e.g., Industry Growth/Public Holding): users type a number like "12.5" and the stored value is treated consistently as a percent value for validation/shareable link.
Validation for this field rejects values outside 0%–100% using a clear English error message.

file_operations.description: Update the Promoter Pledge Quantity input to behave like existing percent fields: label as "Promoter Pledge Quantity (%)", display using toPercentString, and parse using parsePercentInput.
Change promoterPledgeQuantity validation from non-negative numeric to percent-range validation (0%–100%) with a clear English error message.

Keep the existing query parameter key for promoter pledge ("pledge"), but encode/decode it consistently as a percent-style stored value matching other percent fields; add backward-tolerant parsing so existing shared links do not break unexpectedly.
Update DcfInputs defaults/examples for promoterPledgeQuantity to represent a percentage field consistently with other percent inputs (stored as a decimal), and adjust any comments accordingly.

requirements.summary: Rename the UI label from “Result Updated Since 6 Years” to “Result Updated Since 6 Months” without changing underlying state/link keys.
acceptanceCriteria: The Company Snapshot form shows the label "Result Updated Since 6 Months".
The Yes/No control continues to work with the existing state key and shareable link behavior (existing URLs still load correctly).

file_operations.description: Update the displayed label text to "Result Updated Since 6 Months" while keeping the existing state field/key (resultUpdatedSince6Years) unchanged so shareable link behavior remains compatible.

requirements.summary: Convert Net NPA (NBFC) to a percentage field with % label, percent-style parsing/display, and 0–100% validation while preserving the existing query key.
acceptanceCriteria: The Company Snapshot field label reads "Net NPA (NBFC) (%)" (no currency symbol).
The input behavior matches existing percentage fields: users type a number like "3.2" to represent 3.2%.
Validation for this field rejects values outside 0%–100% using a clear English error message.
The shareable link encoding/decoding preserves the Net NPA value correctly under the existing query parameter key (no broken links).

file_operations.description: Update Net NPA (NBFC) field to be a percentage input: label as "Net NPA (NBFC) (%)", display using toPercentString, and parse using parsePercentInput (remove any currency symbol usage).
Change netNpaNbfc validation to percent-range validation (0%–100%) with a clear English error message specific to Net NPA (NBFC).

Preserve the existing query parameter key for Net NPA ("npa"), but encode/decode it consistently as a percentage-stored decimal like other percent fields; include backward-tolerant parsing to avoid breaking existing shared links.
Update DcfInputs defaults/examples for netNpaNbfc to represent a percentage field consistently with other percent inputs (stored as a decimal), and update any comments describing the field.
